summary: Updated hosted macOS CI to install jq explicitly through Homebrew alongside swiftformat and swiftlint before running `just macos-check`, so the shared `just check` path can run the qd report validator without depending on runner-image jq availability.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Hosted macOS CI installs `jq` explicitly alongside the Homebrew-managed tools needed by `just macos-check`.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml now runs `brew install jq swiftformat swiftlint` before `just macos-check`.
The only workflow behavior change is in the macOS Homebrew install line; no Nix files or Linux job commands were changed.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Workflow syntax and repo hygiene checks pass.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `nix develop -c actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml` and `git diff --check` passed; qd report and full check results are recorded in evidence/m4-macos-ci-jq-tooling/commands.log.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence records workflow validation and qd reports.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-macos-ci-jq-tooling/commands.log records workflow validation, and this completion report plus audit report are under reports/qd/m4-macos-ci-jq-tooling/.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product runtime, capture, encode, transport, decode, render, presentation, cross-device, or latency claim.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-macos-ci-jq-tooling/notes.md scopes the work to hosted macOS CI tooling only.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node changes GitHub Actions tool installation only; workflow linting, qd report validation, and repo hygiene checks cover the acceptance without making product runtime claims.
